items.article_h1: n8n Releases Version 2.17.7 with Updated Google Gemini Node Defaults
items.article_standfirst: The open-source workflow automation platform n8n shipped version 2.17.7 on April 24, 2026, updating the default models used by its Google Gemini integration node.
items.article_body: n8n, the workflow automation platform popular among developers and technical teams, released version 2.17.7 on April 24, 2026. The release is tagged as the new stable version on the project's GitHub releases page and marks a point release focused on the platform's native integration with Google's Gemini AI models. The key change in this version is an update to the default model settings for the Google Gemini Node, identified by pull request #28939 and commit 0c856bf.
article_why_matters: Workflows built on n8n's Gemini Node may behave differently after upgrading, since default model choices directly affect output quality and cost.
Keeping default models current is important for teams relying on AI-powered automation to stay competitive without manual reconfiguration.
As a stable release, 2.17.7 is the recommended version for production deployments, making this a noteworthy update for operational teams.
items.article_source_note: This information is sourced directly from the official n8n GitHub releases page (github.com/n8n-io/n8n/releases/tag/stable) and its associated Atom feed, with the release timestamp of 2026-04-24T07:59:04Z confirming today's publication. The release note provides the version number, release date, and specific feature change but lacks extended detail on what new default models were selected.
